rationale: The citing paper gives Van Heerde, Leeflang, and Wittink (2000) a central role in both framing and method. In the introduction, it labels that paper a methodological breakthrough for detecting pre- and postpromotion dips in store data and explicitly positions its own research as the next step called for by that study. In the methods section, it states that it borrows from the methodology pioneered by Van Heerde et al. and bases its lead-lag specification on their model, then later compares results against their findings.
relation_description: The citing paper builds directly on Van Heerde et al. (2000) as the core methodological and conceptual starting point for measuring promotion dips in store scanner data. It extends that line by applying the approach at large UPC-store scale and explaining cross-sectional variation in dip elasticities using UPC, category, and store-trading-area characteristics.
